channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Germany blames ‘sabotage’ as two undersea fiber cables cut in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The German government has blamed an act of sabotage for the cutting of two important undersea fiber optic cables, one connecting connecting Finland and Germany, and the other linking Sweden and Lithuania, on Sunday and Monday. The first cable break happened at around 8 a.m. GMT (3 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What to know about Howard Lutnick, Trump's pick for commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Howard Lutnick, head of brokerage and investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald and cryptocurrency enthusiast, as his nominee for commerce secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Study: Chat GPT is better than doctors at diagnosing illness
item.description: A new research study indicates that Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT-4 is better at diagnosing diseases than human doctors, according to The New York Times. Fifty doctors, a mix of attending physicians and residents, participated in the study; diagnoses were based on evaluations of medical patient cases.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The 3 next big things in robotics and automation for 2024
item.description: Cars still can’t drive themselves everywhere a human can. Robots can’t replace every human manufacturing worker. But those stubborn facts should not distract us from the interesting and creative work being done to expand the ability of robots and drones to help humans do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: The 5 next big things in commerce technology for 2024
item.description: Years after e-commerce rose to unprecedented prominence during the pandemic, virtually every aspect of sales infrastructure, channel, and experience has been targeted by innovation.
item.dc:creator: Ross Rubin, Fast Company

item.title: Fast Company Next Big Things in Tech: general excellence winners
item.description: Among the 130-plus honorees in Fast Company’s Next Big Things in Tech for 2024, four organizations stood out for the ingenuity and potential impact of their innovations.
item.dc:creator: Fast Company Staff, Fast Company

item.title: These AI-assisted innovations are from startups that are five years old or less
item.description: The Googles and Microsofts of the world hardly have a monopoly on innovation. Indeed, a number of recent tech breakthroughs have been delivered from organizations that aren’t even five years old.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: The 5 next big things in building and real estate technology for 2024
item.description: To be blunt, construction projects are a pain. Ongoing labor shortages, cash flow issues, poor communication, and a strained supply chain are all weighing on the industry and can set projects back months. There are also the issues of waste and environmental changes due to climate change to address.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: These midsized companies bring fresh thinking to quietly important jobs
item.description: Equipment has always needed maintenance. We’ve been booking travel online for years. Even using speech recognition to create on-screen captions is not exactly a breakthrough new technology.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Comprehensive data management for AI: The next-gen data management engine that will drive AI to new heights
item.description: All industries and modern applications are undergoing rapid transformation powered by advances in accelerated computing, deep learning, and artificial intelligence. The next phase of this transformation requires an intelligent data infrastructure that can bring AI closer to enterprise data.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House
item.description: Lawmakers axed language in the House-passed Dole Act that set higher standards for the VA to resume the rollout of its troubled new Electronic Health Record. The post Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Cato Networks adds TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Critical VMware Bug Exploited in Wild, Patches Available Now
item.description: VMware bug exploited in wild, patches available, threat actors targeting compromised networks
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Foxconn takes another big step toward AI iPhone manufacturing
item.description: Apple’s main manufacturing partner, Foxconn, has announced it is working with Nvidia to build digital twins that it says will reshape the future of manufacturing and supply chain management.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft brings automated ‘agents’ to M365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new tool in Microsoft 365 Copilot to automate repetitive tasks, part of a drive to make the generative AI (genAI) assistant more useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Matt Gaetz could launch Big Tech crackdown at DOJ
item.description: President-elect Trump’s decision to nominate former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) as attorney general signals a rough road ahead for major technology firms during his second administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Instagram rolls out option to reset recommendations
item.description: Instagram is testing a new feature that allows users to reset their content recommendations on the platform when in need of a "fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Data breach exposes over 56 million clothing store customers
item.description: Breach site confirmed 56,904,909 Hot Topic users' data leaked online. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the company's silence makes matters even worse.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: He aquí 11 maneras de garantizar la alineación entre TI y Negocio
item.description: La mayoría de los ejecutivos de TI y de negocios reconocen la necesidad de una alineación estrecha. Entienden que sus estrategias, capacidades, recursos y sistemas de gestión deben configurarse para respaldar el propósito y los objetivos generales de la empresa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A beautiful machine: one week with an M4 Pro MacBook Pro
item.description: The new MacBook Pro is everything you expect: faster, better and more capable than before. While you can say that about every new Mac, the move to Apple Silicon means Apple can introduce vastly improved systems almost every year — something that was not always possible before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to “act on our behalf across our work and life.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NIST’s quantum standards: The time for upgrades is now
item.description: Quantum computing is farther along than most realize, and it's time to safeguard against cybersecurity threats posed by those who will use quantum technologies. The post NIST’s quantum standards: The time for upgrades is now first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump expected to attend SpaceX launch on Tuesday
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk. The launch is scheduled for 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reimagines Fabric with focus on AI
item.description: Microsoft announced a host of additions to its Microsoft Fabric data analytics platform at Microsoft Ignite 2024 in Chicago on Tuesday, including Fabric Databases.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 4 monkeys remain free nearly 2 weeks after dozens escaped a South Carolina compound
item.description: Authorities say four monkeys remain free nearly two weeks after a group of 43 escaped from a South Carolina compound that breeds them for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top 8 failings in delivering value with generative AI and how to overcome them
item.description: Generative AI (GenAI) is rapidly emerging as a game changer for enterprises, but turning its potential into measurable value remains a significant challenge.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google Faces Historic Antitrust Action from DOJ: Implications for Chrome and AI Ventures
item.description: DOJ proposes historic antitrust action against Google, aiming to reshape tech landscape by forcing sale of Chrome browser and regulating AI data usage
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Organizations Prioritize Short-Term IT Goals Over Long-Term Innovation: A Growing Concern for CEOs and IT Leaders
item.description: CEOs are increasingly prioritizing short-term IT goals over long-term innovation, according to a recent survey. This trend has significant implications for organizations and their ability to adapt to changing market conditions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A GRC framework for securing generative AI
item.description: From automating workflows to unlocking new insights, generative AI models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 are already delivering value in enterprises across every industry.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Succeeding with observability in the cloud
item.description: In its 2024 report, Splunk breaks down observability practices into four stages: foundational visibility, guided insights, proactive response, and unified workflows. Based on that, it classifies companies into four stages of observability maturity: beginning, emerging, evolving, and leading.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
